source.type: frontend-slides creative-mode adapted into slides-generator artifacts

direction.personality: Confident, practical and founder-friendly, with a bold printed-zine energy rather than a generic corporate pitch.
direction.audience_fit: Chleo Lee and supporters evaluating whether a Singapore community band room can become a real studio without rushing into lease risk.
direction.density: One hard message per slide, supported by oversized headings, compact cards, source rails and visual matrices.
direction.depth: Operational enough to answer demand generation, legal gates, property scouting, fitout, gear, quotes, startup cash and 90-day execution.
direction.grid: Fixed 1920 x 1080 stage scaled uniformly to desktop and mobile viewports; no responsive reflow inside slides.

patterns.title_slide: Use a poster-style opening with a large founder-specific title and a simple studio switch visual.
patterns.section_slide: Use a large uppercase claim plus a compact visual system that proves the next action.
patterns.comparison_slide: Use hard-bordered cards, common-axis bars and clear recommendation labels.
patterns.table_slide: Use tables only for legality/property matrices, with large cells and source footers.
patterns.architecture_slide: Use flow cards, layer cards, gates and timeline matrices to show execution paths.

must_preserve: A recommendation to validate before lease
Specific customer-discovery pools and quote categories
Presenter controls, overview and speaker notes
accessibility: High contrast cream and ink palette
No text or component bounds outside the slide

decisions.decision: Use Creative Mode as the primary visual system.
decisions.rationale: It creates a memorable, polished presentation while staying light, friendly and not nightclub-like.

decisions.decision: Keep a fixed 16:9 stage on mobile.
decisions.rationale: The deck is meant to be presented as slides; uniform scaling prevents the previous wrapping and scrolling failures.
Demand, site permission and noise risk are the major cash-risk gates for a first studio.
